message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Tasks 5-6 done, proceed with Task 7">
Status update:

**Tasks 5 and 6 are COMPLETE.** All a11y fixes applied, CI green on `6c757bc`.

**Task 7 (View todo list + Add todo) is now the active task.** I see some of you have already started domain types for todos (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, etc.) in the working tree. That's the right next task — just make sure you follow the commit discipline:

1. Get to green → commit → push → verify CI
2. Do NOT accumulate uncommitted work
3. One thing at a time

**Commit discipline rules**: We have 8/9 consents (everyone except Carson and Luca). That's well past consensus. **Kent: please add the Commit Discipline section to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and commit it now.** Carson and Luca can register concerns after the fact if they have any.

**Carson, Luca**: Your consent votes are still welcome but we're proceeding with adoption.

Let's go. Task 7 — view todo list and add a todo item.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Task 7 is active. My design specs for this are already sent to Carson (both the original and the updated version with Heydon's a11y corrections). Let me also make sure the site header CSS gets into `main.css` since Task 7 is the first authenticated page and needs the header with logout.

content.text: Clean working tree, CI green. Let me check what the current `main.css` looks like to see if Lea already added the site header CSS.
Site header CSS hasn't been added yet. Task 7 needs it since it's the first authenticated page. Let me add the site header and todo list component CSS to `main.css` now.
I'll add the site header, button--text, todo add form, todo list, and empty state CSS right before the closing `}` of the components layer.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: +   * Authenticated page header with brand link and user actions (logout).
+   * Flexbox layout: brand left, actions right. */
+   * Button styled as a text link. Used for logout and other
+   * non-primary actions that are semantically form submissions. */
+   * Prominent input at top of todo list for adding new items.
+   * Label above, input + button row below. */
+   * Vertical list of todo items separated by borders.
+   * Each item is a flex row: checkbox + label (title). */
+  /* Custom checkbox -- native checkbox with appearance stripped */
+  /* Completed items: strikethrough + muted color (two visual indicators) */

content.text: CSS is in. Let me notify the team.

input.content:
I've added the Task 7 component CSS to `static/css/main.css` in the components layer. New components added:

- `.site-header`, `.site-header__brand`, `.site-header__actions` -- authenticated page header
- `.button--text` -- text-styled button for logout (includes `cursor: pointer` and `border: none` per your suggestion)
- `.todo-add`, `.todo-add__label`, `.todo-add__row`, `.todo-add__input`, `.todo-add__button` -- add todo form
- `.todo-list`, `.todo-item`, `.todo-item__toggle`, `.todo-item__title`, `.todo-item--completed` -- todo list items
- `.todo-item__toggle input[type="checkbox"]` -- custom checkbox styling with `appearance: none`
- `.todo-empty` -- empty state message

Please review when you get a chance. This is unstaged -- it needs to be committed per the commit discipline. I didn't add a `:focus-visible` style for `.button--text` since that was on your queue -- feel free to add it alongside this or as a follow-up.
input.summary: Added Task 7 component CSS, please review

routing.summary: Added Task 7 component CSS, please review

input.content:
Task 7 component CSS is now in `static/css/main.css`. All the classes from the design spec are ready to use:

- Site header: `.site-header`, `.site-header__brand`, `.site-header__actions`
- Logout button: `.button--text`
- Add form: `.todo-add`, `.todo-add__label`, `.todo-add__row`, `.todo-add__input`, `.todo-add__button`
- Todo list: `.todo-list`, `.todo-item`, `.todo-item__toggle`, `.todo-item__title`
- Completed state: `.todo-item--completed`
- Custom checkbox: `.todo-item__toggle input[type="checkbox"]`
- Empty state: `.todo-empty`

This needs to be committed. Someone driving the Task 7 TDD cycle can include it in their next green commit.

content.text:
Task 7 is in progress. I've added the component CSS for the site header, todo list, add form, checkbox, and empty state. The CSS is unstaged and waiting to be included in a commit by whoever is driving the TDD cycle.

Let me check the current state to see if there's been progress on the Rust side.

content.text:
Good -- significant progress. There's 1 unpushed commit, and the working tree has todo domain types, repository, service, routes, auth middleware, templates, and a migration. The CSS also has a small update I see -- a comment clarification on `--color-success` noting it fails AA for normal text (which is correct per Heydon's earlier audit).

This is accumulating a lot of uncommitted work again. But the team lead just reminded everyone about commit discipline, so I trust the driver is working toward a green state to commit.

Let me review the new template to check design compliance.

content.text: I can see the template is a good start but has several gaps from the design spec. Let me send feedback to the driver.

input.content:
Reviewing `templates/todos.html` -- good start, but several items need attention to match the design spec: **1. Header/nav block**: The nav block has a standalone logout form but is missing the full site header layout. Should be: ```html {% block nav %}
Todo List
{{ user_email }}
{% endblock %} ``` Uses `.site-header` (brand left, actions right) and `.button--text` (text-styled logout button). Currently using `.nav-logout` and `.nav-logout__button` which don't exist in the CSS. **2. Add form class names**: Template uses `.todo-add__group` and `.todo-add__submit` -- the CSS has `.todo-add__row` and `.todo-add__button`. Also the label needs `class="todo-add__label"` and the input needs `id="new-todo"` and `class="todo-add__input"`. Updated structure: ```html
``` **3. Missing checkbox**: Todo items currently only show the title. They need the toggle checkbox per the design spec. This is needed for Task 8 (complete a todo) but the form structure should be in place now: ```html
    • ``` **4. Button text**: "Add todo" should be just "Add" -- shorter is better, the label already says "New todo". These are all class name alignment issues and structural gaps. The overall page structure (heading, form at top, list below, empty state) is correct.
input.summary: Todos template review: 4 design alignment issues

routing.content:
Reviewing `templates/todos.html` -- good start, but several items need attention to match the design spec: **1. Header/nav block**: The nav block has a standalone logout form but is missing the full site header layout. Should be: ```html {% block nav %}
Todo List
{{ user_email }}
{% endblock %} ``` Uses `.site-header` (brand left, actions right) and `.button--text` (text-styled logout button). Currently using `.nav-logout` and `.nav-logout__button` which don't exist in the CSS. **2. Add form class names**: Template uses `.todo-add__group` and `.todo-add__submit` -- the CSS has `.todo-add__row` and `.todo-add__button`. Also the label needs `class="todo-add__label"` and the input needs `id="new-todo"` and `class="todo-add__input"`. Updated structure: ```html
``` **3. Missing checkbox**: Todo items currently only show the title. They need the toggle checkbox per the design spec. This is needed for Task 8 (complete a todo) but the form structure should be in place now: ```html
    • ``` **4. Button text**: "Add todo" should be just "Add" -- shorter is better, the label already says "New todo". These are all class name alignment issues and structural gaps. The overall page structure (heading, form at top, list below, empty state) is correct.

content.text: Design review sent. The template needs alignment with the CSS class names I've added and the structural patterns from the design spec. Waiting for the driver to incorporate these changes.
